name: create_transaction
description: Create a sale transaction to charge a payment method. You can use a payment method nonce (from the client SDK), a vaulted payment method token, or a customer ID (charges their default payment method). The transaction can be submitted for settlement immediately or authorized and captured later. Supports partial settlements, service fees for marketplaces, and 3D Secure verification.
note: Braintree uses a GraphQL API. The legacy REST API endpoint is POST /merchants/{merchant_id}/transactions. Both are supported.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: The amount to charge in the settlement currency. Must be a string with two decimal places.

parameters.name: payment_method_nonce
parameters.description: A one-time-use nonce from the Braintree client SDK representing the customer's payment method. Use this for new payments from the Drop-in UI or Hosted Fields.

parameters.name: payment_method_token
parameters.description: A token referencing a vaulted (stored) payment method. Use this for returning customers.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: The vault customer ID. If provided without a payment method, the customer's default payment method is charged.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Transaction options. Set submit_for_settlement to true to immediately settle, or false to just authorize.

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: Your order or invoice ID for this transaction.

parameters.name: device_data
parameters.description: Device data from the Braintree client SDK for fraud detection.

parameters.name: billing
parameters.description: Billing address for AVS (Address Verification System) checks.

body.query: mutation ChargePaymentMethod($input: ChargePaymentMethodInput!) { chargePaymentMethod(input: $input) { transaction { id status amount { value currencyCode } } } }
